evals.prompt: We've decided every uploaded file must pass a safety check before any DDP validation or extraction runs. Record this as a lean ADR that will route to the upload-handling Python code (port/**/*.py) so future edits there get the rule.
evals.expected_output: A lean ADR authored with `adg lean new` at docs/decisions/NNNN-slug.md: status accepted, a one-screen Decision + Guidance body (no MADR Context/Considered Options/Decision Outcome), priority invariant (a safety rule), and applies_to routing covering the upload code (e.g. port/**/*.py). Created with `adg lean new` (which validates the candidate before writing and refuses on a hard failure), not by hand and not with `adg add` (the MADR command); re-validates clean with `adg lean index --model docs/decisions` (0 failures).

assertions.description: A lean ADR NNNN-slug.md exists under docs/decisions
assertions.check: docs/decisions/ contains a new NNNN-slug.md matching the decision

assertions.description: The ADR routes to the upload code
assertions.check: frontmatter sets applies_to to a glob covering port/ Python files (e.g. port/**/*.py), and priority is invariant

assertions.description: The body is lean, not MADR
assertions.check: body has ## Decision and ## Guidance and does NOT use MADR sections (Context and Problem Statement, Considered Options, Decision Outcome)

assertions.description: adg lean index validates the model
assertions.check: adg lean index --model docs/decisions reports 0 failures for the new record

assertions.description: Authored with `adg lean new`, not by hand and not `adg add`
assertions.check: summary shows the record was created with `adg lean new` (which validates before writing), not hand-written and not via `adg add` (the MADR command)
